source: documents/scotus-amicus-brief.pdf (US Supreme Court amicus brief No.21-659, 20p, pure legal prose)
note: No tables, formulas or images -> all weight goes to Structure & Text (the other dimensions are 'na' and excluded, not scored as 0). Structure & Text = avg(headings / reading-order junctions / content anchors / footnote survival). The original brief contains its own sic typos (QUESTIONS PRESENTRED / TABLE OF AUTHORITHIES / Unites States); these are tagged source and not charged to the converter.

headings._desc: 14 headings: 8 level-1 (cover title + QUESTION PRESENTED + TOC + TOA + IDENTITY/INTEREST + SUMMARY + REASONS FOR GRANTING + CONCLUSION) + 4 TOA sub-heads (CASES/CONST/STATUTES/RULES) + 2 argument sub-sections (I./II.).

anchors._desc: Key legal facts that must survive verbatim. The probe mechanically checks presence; whether a value was silently changed must be verified by eye.

items.probe: Supreme Court of the United States
Asociaci[oó]n de Periodistas de Puerto Rico
Overseas Press Club of Puerto Rico

junctions.J1_footnotes_not_inlined: The 11 bottom-of-page footnotes must not be spliced into the superscript-carrying body sentences; in particular footnote 1 (p.1, two paragraphs of Rule 37.2/37.6 disclosure) and the short citation footnote (Richmond... at foot of p.3) must not be sewn into the sentence above.
junctions.J2_toa_dotleaders: The Table of Authorities (pp.iv-v) is a dot-leader list, not a data table: each entry is case name + dot leaders + page number and must not be scattered; the CASES/CONST/STATUTES/RULES grouping must not be jumbled.
junctions.J3_toc_entries: In the Table of Contents (p.iii) each entry name must line up with its page number (ii/iv/1/2/3/5/7), and I./II. must stay indented under REASONS.
junctions.J4_cross_page: Body paragraphs must rejoin seamlessly across pages, skipping the centered page number and the bottom-of-page footnote block.
junctions.J5_signature_block: The two-column signature block (cover + p.15) must not interleave the two attorneys' addresses line by line into one tangle; 'Respectfully submitted' / 'DATED' must stay above the block.

footnotes._desc: 11 numbered footnotes must each survive as a whole, not vanish and not melt into the body text. Footnote 1 = a substantive two-paragraph disclosure (highest risk of being lost entirely); 2-11 = legal citations (incl. Id./supra).
